body:
Hi Hila,

I've attached the code review (see comments starting with "CR").
The solution looks good overall, hope you had fun.
We actually have DNS sniffer which is not that different from your
solution, this represents some of the work we do in SAM.

If you have any questions feel free to ask.

Thanks,
Colleague A

On Tue, Dec 5, 2017 at 11:59 AM, Colleague A <
> wrote:

> Hi Hila,
>
> Thanks for the response.
> Colleague B will contact you in the beginning of next week (with feedback
> regarding the exercise as well).
>
> Thanks again,
> Colleague A
>
>
> On Sun, Dec 3, 2017 at 10:29 PM, Hila Shmuel <> wrote:
>
>>
>>
>> On Sat, Dec 2, 2017 at 8:03 PM, Colleague A <
>> > wrote:
>>
>>> Yes, that's fine.
>>>
>>> On Dec 2, 2017 7:50 PM, "Hila Shmuel" <> wrote:
>>>
>>>> hey
>>>>
>>>> it was really fun till i had to parse DNS by myself.
>>>> can i use pcap or something like that?
>>>>
>>>> On Thu, Nov 30, 2017 at 6:20 PM, Colleague A <
>>>> > wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Hi Hila,
>>>>>
>>>>> As Colleague B said, a small programming exercise.
>>>>>
>>>>> Please write a program (in C) that sniffs DNS response packets and
>>>>> prints the domain and IPs resolved for that domain.
>>>>> You can ignore IPv6, the code safe and to the point.
>>>>>
>>>>> Have fun :)
>>>>>
>>>>> --
>>>>> Thanks,
>>>>> Colleague A
>>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>
>
>
> --
> Thanks,
> Colleague A
>



-- 
Thanks,
Colleague A
explanation: The code utilizes raw sockets to listen for incoming UDP packets on port 53, which is the standard port for DNS queries and responses. When a DNS response packet is captured, it parses the packet to extract information such as source and destination IP addresses, DNS inquiry details, and the resolved IP addresses of the requested domain. It follows RFC 1035 to properly parse and interpret the DNS packet structure, allowing it to display relevant information in a human-readable format.
name: 2017-12-03_home-exercise
subject: Re: Home exercise
summary: The project implements a DNS response packet sniffer in C that captures DNS responses and prints the resolved domain names along with their associated IP addresses.
